title: padus avium var. aucubifolia
intro: Padus avium var. aucubifolia, also known as bird cherry or mountain cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Padus avium var. aucubifolia include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health, making it a valuable herb in the treatment of various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
